item.title: Incidence and prevalence of inflammatory Bowel disease in China: a regional population-based study from 2020 to 2022.
item.description: Scandinavian journal of gastroenterology - The global burden of inflammatory bowel diseases (IBDs), comprising Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC), is on the rise. Studies regarding the incidence of IBD in China remain notably ...

item.title: Predictive value of iron deficiency anaemia on the one-year disease course in inflammatory bowel disease patients in clinical and biochemical remission.
item.description: Scandinavian journal of gastroenterology - Iron deficiency anaemia (IDA) is a common comorbidity in inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD). It is hypothesized that IDA result in poorer outcomes also in patients with remission. This multicenter r...

item.title: Association of frail status with incident digestive diseases: A large prospective cohort study.
item.description: Digestive diseases (Basel, Switzerland) - Frailty, reflecting age-related decline in physiological reserve, has been implicated in adverse health outcomes, but its contribution to digestive disease risk is not well established. Leveraging ...

item.title: The trend of obesity and inflammatory bowel disease in Australia and New Zealand: a bi-nation cohort study.
item.description: Internal medicine journal - The paradigm of inflammatory bowel disease is shifting from a malabsorptive, undernourished state to one where obesity is a prevalent comorbidity, yet large-scale Australasian data are lacking. We ...

item.title: Safety of IL-17A Inhibitors in Patients With Moderate to Severe Psoriasis in a US Claims Data-Based Cohort Study.
item.description: Journal of psoriasis and psoriatic arthritis - Safety outcomes from IL-17A inhibitors have not been fully evaluated. Compare the risk of seven safety outcomes in patients with psoriasis who started treatment with either ixekizumab or secukinuma...

item.title: Trends and disparities in the burden of autoimmune diseases in China and globally: A comparative analysis based on the Global Burden of Disease Study 2021.
item.description: Medicine - This study aimed to quantify long-term trends and burdens of 5 autoimmune diseases in China and globally from 1990 to 2021 using Global Burden of Disease 2021 data and identify drivers of change to...

item.title: Crohn's Disease Activity Index in the era of obesity: time to abandon it?
item.description: Journal of Crohn's & colitis - Obesity and metabolic syndrome are now common among patients with Crohn's disease, with prevalence rates approaching those of the general population. Yet the Crohn's Disease Activity Index (CDAI), ...

item.title: Pharmacological interventions for obesity in patients with inflammatory bowel disease: A systematic review of GLP-1 receptor agonist efficacy and safety.
item.description: Obesity pillars - Review - Obesity is an escalating global health challenge, particularly among patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), where the coexistence of metabolic dysfunction and chronic intestinal inflammati...
